explanation: This command uses the -s and -i flags as specified in the Sigma rule, searching for the term 'subfolder' in a case-insensitive manner across all .txt files in the System32 directory and subdirectories.
This command substitutes short flags (-s, -i) with their equivalent long form (/s, /i), which is a valid mutation technique and still matches the Sigma rule.
This command reorders the flags (-i and -s), which is a valid mutation technique and still matches the Sigma rule.
This command omits output by redirecting it to NUL, which is a valid mutation technique and still matches the Sigma rule.
This command uses an environment variable (%windir%) to represent the Windows directory, which is a valid mutation technique and still matches the Sigma rule.
